justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Manual restart requirement during failover clearly identified as critical operational gap and hard blocker, (2) Dynamic primary resolution recommended as the fix (cluster endpoint, DNS, service discovery), (3) No retry on connection error identified as a reliability gap with specific recommendation (1-2 retries, 100-500ms backoff), (4) Read replica routing identified correctly as a should-have optimization rather than a blocker, (5) Verdict is NOT READY, (6) Five specific must-have recommendations plus additional should-haves provided. No failure modes triggered.

observations.text: Correctly categorized read replica routing as a should-have (optimization) rather than a blocker, matching the rubric's guidance that it is not a reliability blocker
Exceptionally detailed resilience assessment covering five distinct failure scenarios with specific handling status and recommendations
Provided concrete, actionable recommendations with effort estimates and clear prioritization (blockers vs should-haves vs nice-to-haves)
Identified additional valuable gaps beyond the rubric: connection acquisition timeout, statement_timeout, pool exhaustion metrics, circuit breaker absence
Appropriate escalation routing to MJ for architecture-level decisions on connection resolution and read/write splitting
Bottom line summary clearly articulates the two critical gaps and provides a concrete path to READY WITH CAVEATS
